beats.activity: Wake up, check on IPA fermentation, morning coffee and toast
beats.reasoning: Cyrus is a creature of habit; brewery monitoring is part of his morning ritual. Energy at 50/100 suggests a moderate start—nothing rushed, just his established routine.

beats.activity: Scenic drive to work via Ballard Bridge
beats.reasoning: Cyrus always takes the scenic route and loves the Ballard Bridge. Friday morning commute allows time to enjoy the drive; warm 77F weather makes it an especially pleasant journey.

beats.activity: Deep work on Magnolia park expansion renderings and design tweaks
beats.reasoning: Friday morning is prime focused-work time before the weekend. Cyrus gets into flow state with design work; no meetings scheduled allows for uninterrupted productivity on the renderings he's been refining.

beats.activity: Lunch at Solstice Pizza, quiet corner table
beats.reasoning: Cyrus has established Solstice as his go-to lunch spot; the wood-fired oven and quietness suit his preference for small moments of calm during the workday. On the way home from Interbay, it's a natural detour.

beats.activity: Scenic drive back to Interbay via Fremont, eyeing green spaces
beats.reasoning: Cyrus takes the scenic route; warm Friday weather and recent rains make him alert for mushroom activity, so he loops through Fremont mindfully even though it's a detour.

beats.activity: Afternoon work: client check-ins and final design polish
beats.reasoning: Friday afternoon has lighter energy; wrapping up the week with polished renderings and any final client feedback. By 4:30pm, Cyrus is ready to leave for the day.

beats.activity: Quick mushroom scout at Woodland Park south entrance before heading home
beats.reasoning: Warm, breezy Friday with recent rains = prime mushroom conditions. Cyrus detours to Woodland Park on his way home; foraging is his cherished free-time activity and the timing is perfect before evening.

beats.activity: Drive home to Fremont, avoiding Capitol Hill road closures
beats.reasoning: Seattle Pride street fair and road closures on Broadway/Pike from 5pm onward. Cyrus, skeptical of big crowds and valuing quiet time, deliberately avoids downtown. Route via Fremont keeps him clear of chaos.

beats.activity: Dinner: simple meal at home, check on any foraged mushrooms from earlier
beats.reasoning: Cyrus prefers quiet evenings at home over big Pride crowds. He cooks a simple dinner and processes any mushrooms found at Woodland Park—a meditative ritual that fits his energy and values.

beats.activity: Home brewery work: monitor IPA fermentation, prep ingredients for next batch, light reading
beats.reasoning: Friday evening wind-down. Cyrus tends to his brewing passion—checking the IPA he's been monitoring all week, planning the next batch. This meditative, solitary activity suits his mood and moderate energy.
Cyrus heads to bed around 10pm for a full night's rest. The sleep beat spans overnight, ending at 6:30am Saturday morning, allowing him to wake refreshed for the weekend.

world_event_prompt: Warm, breezy Friday, high of 77F. Seattle Pride kickoff: Capitol Hill street fair and road closures along Broadway and Pike from 5pm. Festive, busy evening downtown.
